item.title: 作为开发人员，您在 2022 年的首要任务是什么？ | What is your top priority as a developer in 2022?
item.description:
开发团队的Ben Halpern

发表于这是 DEV 和Mayfield之间设计的一系列大问题中的第一篇。

从这个开始，请每天参与一个新的线程。 经过 10 次讨论后，我们将总结一些讨论并发布关键要点。

快乐编码！



原文:

Ben Halpern for The DEV Team

Posted on May 9

This is the first post in a series of big questions devised between DEV and Mayfield.

Please feel welcome to take part in a new thread every day starting with this one. After a 10 discussions we will be rounding up some of the discussions and publishing key takeaways.

Happy coding!
item.author: ben

item.title: 音乐星期一——你在听什么？ (May 9) （5月9日） | Music Monday — What are you listening to? (May 9)
item.description:
封面图片来源： DLGNCE

在这个每周系列中，人们可以加入并删除他们正在收听的任何内容的链接 + 浏览其他建议。

如果您喜欢谈论音乐，请考虑关注 org #music 讨论以获取更多关于音乐的对话！

同样值得注意的是，我的朋友@duxtech在这里有这个系列的西班牙语版本，以防你也想插话。 那么，你们今天都在听什么？ 注意：您可以使用以下语法在大多数平台上嵌入指向您的歌曲的链接{% embed https://... %} 。

期待听听大家的建议！ 技术世界与娜娜 - Apr 6 4月6日



原文:

cover image credits: DLGNCE

In this weekly series, folks can chime in and drop links to whatever it is they're listening to + browse others suggestions.

If you like talking about music, consider following the org #music discussions for more conversations about music!

Also noteworthy, my friend @duxtech has a Spanish speaking version of this series here in case you'd like to chime in there as well. 🙌

So, what have y'all been listening to today? Note: you can embed a link to your song on most platforms using the following syntax {% embed https://... %}.

I look forward to listening to y'all's suggestions! 👂 🎶

TechWorld with Nana - Apr 6
item.author: michaeltharrington

item.title: App Generator - 使用 AppSeed 编写简单的仪表板 | App Generator - Code a simple Dashboard using AppSeed
item.description:
你好编码员！

本文介绍了如何使用AppSeed通过可视化界面生成simple Flask Dashboard 。 用户无需帐户即可访问该服务，根据他们的选择生成一个新项目并从 Github（MIT 许可证）下载代码。

谢谢阅读！

这是解释该过程的 Youtube视频。

任何没有帐户的人都可以使用该生成器，并且可以在 Github 上实时访问源代码。 基于许可许可证，源代码可用于商业项目和电子学习活动。

让我们重申视频中介绍的步骤：

在不到 1 分钟的时间内，源代码应该可以在 Github 上下载。 在本地环境中开始使用可能更简单的方法是使用Docker设置。 要使用该项目，只需在项目的根目录中键入一个命令：

 $ docker-compose up --build

执行命令后，应该可以在浏览器中访问 Datta Able：

Thanks for reading! 如需更多资源，请随时访问：



原文:

Hello coders!

This article explains how to use AppSeed to generate a simple Flask Dashboard using a visual interface. Users can access the service without an account, generate a new project based on their selections and download the code from Github (MIT License).

Thanks for reading!

Here is the Youtube video that explains the process.

✨ How to use the service

The generator can be used by anyone without an account and the sources can be accessed in real-time on Github. Based on the permissive license, source code can be used in commercial projects and eLearning activities.

Let's reiterate the steps presented in the video:

👉 The User selects the design - Datta Able in our DEMO

👉 Select the preferred backend

👉 Configure the product: Database and Deployment

👉 Confirm the action and wait to download the sources:

In less than 1 minute the sources should be available on Github for download. Probably the easier way to get started in a local environment is to use the Docker set up. To use the project, just type a single command in the root of the project:

$ docker-compose up --build

Once the command is executed, Datta Able should be accessible in the browser:

Thanks for reading! For more resources, feel free to access:
item.author: sm0ke

item.title: OOP 的科学证据是大规模精神病 | The scientific proof of that OOP is a mass psychosis
item.description:
由于我之前的文章产生了 100 多条评论、9,500 次页面浏览量，以及一些愤怒地评论要求我的陈述证明的人，我想我会证明这一点。 请注意，我试图通过说明“证明就在您的代码中”来解释 OOP 的证明是如何成为软件开发大规模精神病的 - 但是，显然没有人相信我，所以让我用两个简短的代码片段来说明我的意思做的基本一样。

超λ

 join fork http.get:"https://gaiasoul.com" fork http.get:"https://servergardens.com"

对于我们这些没有偏见的人来说，上述内容应该是不言自明的，足以作为证据。 但是，如果您仍然觉得很难看证明，请向孩子（尚未接触 OOP）解释多线程的基础知识，然后解释 HTTP 和从其他服务器检索文档的基础知识，并询问孩子“哪个最容易阅读” ？

如果您仍有疑问，请意识到上述 C++ 代码是由评论者提供的，以证明 C++ “与函数式编程语言一样好” 。 然后意识到没有m_Factories变量的实例化。 假设代码使用公认的编码标准，假设它是某种成员变量可能是安全的，这意味着它声明的类没有显示，成员变量本身也没有显示，所以假设 (完整）代码示例可能至少是上面 C++ 示例中所示大小的 3/4 倍。 当然，C++ 示例也有大量额外的不必要的构造，例如类、类型、构造函数，可能是析构函数，甚至可能（叹息！）复制构造函数等等——这些额外开销中的大部分当然是额外不必要的它与所有 OOP 语言共享的开销。 但是，即使忽略缺少的 C++ 部分，以下内容显然是正确的……

然后意识到上面的Hyperlambda代码片段可以直接粘贴到“Eval”菜单项中并立即执行，这意味着它是一个 100% 完美完整的示例。 您可以在本文的封面图片中看到一些内容。 最后，还要意识到所有实际问题的 Hyperlambda 片段与 C++ 版本一样快，因为 99.999999% 的时间都花在等待 IO 上，并且 Hyperlambda 片段是 100% 完全async的，因此具有相同的特征就 CPU 和 RAM 使用而言，它的 C++ 等价物。 因此，即使 C++ 有时可能比 Hyperlambda 更快，但 C++ 为您带来的速度性能提升在 99% 的情况下完全无关紧要，除非您正在开发硬件驱动程序或类似的东西。

事实是，我可以将 95% 的现有 OOP 代码替换为 20% 的 Hyperlambda 代码。 如果您使用的 C++ 可能只有 Hyperlambda 代码的 5% - 而且对于所有实际问题，它会同样快，消耗相同数量的内存，并且没有更多的 CPU - 假设您正在创建 Web 应用程序当然，访问某种数据库，与其他 HTTP 服务集成。 但是，OOP 是软件开发人员集体精神病的证据确实在您的代码中，所以为了清楚起见，让我再次重复代码......

超λ

 join fork http.get:"https://gaiasoul.com" fork http.get:"https://servergardens.com"

真的，反对代码实际上是徒劳的......：/

顺便说一句，如果您此时要向我展示 Python、Ruby 或 PHP 代码，请意识到 Hyperlambda 可能（至少）比所有这些语言都快一个数量级......

证明的解释

如果你还是不明白，试着想象一个软件开发菜鸟看着这两个代码片段，问问题。 在理解这两个代码片段之前，菜鸟需要问多少问题。 那么，Hyperlambda 版本将如下所示。

  1. 冒号是怎么回事: ？
  2. 空格怎么了？
  3. 什么是叉子？
  4. 什么是加盟？
  5. 什么是网址？

什么是常量？

  • 分配如何工作？
  • 双冒号::是怎么回事？
  • 什么是加盟？
  • 为什么是括号？
  • 为什么m_Factories以m_开头？
  • 怎么了. 在m_factories之后？
  • 为什么我们不能使用. 在HTTP之后， ->部分是什么？
  • 分号是怎么回事？

    • 事实是，一旦您将 C++ 分解为在使用该语言变得富有成效之前需要得到答案的问题，C++ 只不过是“美化的正则表达式” ，将数百万个字符捆绑​​在一起，因此只需更改其中一个字符，通常会对您的整个程序产生影响。 大多数其他 OOP 语言也是如此......



    原文:

    Since my previous article generated 100+ comments, 9,500 page views, and some people commenting in anger demanding proof of my statements, I figured I'd prove it. Notice, I tried to explain how the proof of that OOP is a software development mass psychosis by stating "the proof is in your code" - However, nobody apparently believed me, so let me illustrate what I meant by that with two short code snippets doing basically the same.

    C++ 

    const auto ret = Tasks::join(
   m_Factories.HTTP->GET("https//gaiasoul.com"),
   m_Factories.HTTP->GET("https://servergardens.com")
).Start();

    Hyperlambda 

    join
   fork
      http.get:"https://gaiasoul.com"
   fork
      http.get:"https://servergardens.com"

    For those of us without bias the above should really be self explanatory and enough as proof. However, if you still find it difficult to see the proof, explain the basics of multi threading to a child (who's still not been exposed to OOP), then explain the basics of HTTP and retrieving documents from other servers, and ask the child "which is the easiest to read"?

    If you're still in doubt, please realise that the above C++ code was supplied by a commenter as proof of that C++ was "just as good as functional programming languages". Then realise there's no instantiation of the m_Factories variable. Assuming the code is using the accepted coding standards, it's probably safe to assume it's a member variable of some sort, implying the class where it's declared is not shown, the member variable itself is also not shown, so it's probably safe to assume the (complete) code example would probably be at least 3/4 times as large as illustrated in the C++ example above. The C++ example of course also has tons of additional unnecessary constructs, such as classes, types, constructors probably, possibly destructors, maybe even (sigh!) copy constructors, etc, etc, etc - Most of this additional overhead of course being additional unnecessary overhead it shares with all OOP languages out there. However, even ignoring the missing C++ parts, the following is obviously true ...

    The Hyperlambda example is obviously 100 times more readable!

    Then realise the Hyperlambda code snippet above can literally be pasted into the "Eval" menu item and immediately executed, implying it's a 100% perfectly complete example. Something you can see in the cover image for this article. Then finally, also realise that the Hyperlambda snippet for all practical concerns is just as fast as the C++ version, since 99.999999% of the time is spent waiting for IO, and the Hyperlambda snippet is 100% perfectly async, and hence shares the same traits in regards to CPU and RAM usage as its C++ equivalent. So even though C++ might sometimes be faster than Hyperlambda, the speed performance gains C++ gives you would in 99% of the times be completely irrelevant unless you're developing drivers for hardware or something similar.

    Facts are, I can replace 95% of your existing OOP code, with 20% as much Hyperlambda code. If you're using C++ probably as little as 5% as much Hyperlambda code - And it'll for all practical concerns be just as fast, consume the same amount of memory, and no more CPU - Assuming you're creating web apps of course, accessing some sort of database, integrating with other HTTP services. However, the proof of that OOP is a software developer mass psychosis is literally in your code, so let me repeat the code once more for clarity reasons ...

    C++ 

    const auto ret = Tasks::join(
   m_Factories.HTTP->GET("https//gaiasoul.com"),
   m_Factories.HTTP->GET("https://servergardens.com")
).Start();

    Hyperlambda 

    join
   fork
      http.get:"https://gaiasoul.com"
   fork
      http.get:"https://servergardens.com"

    Really, arguing against the code is literally futile ... :/

    BTW, in case you're about to show me Python, Ruby, or PHP code at this point, please realise that Hyperlambda is probably (at least) one order of magnitude faster than all of these languages ...

    The explanation of the proof

    If you still don't get it, try to imagine a software development noob looking at these two snippets of code, asking questions. How many questions would the noob need to ask before understanding these two code snippets. Well, the Hyperlambda version would be as follows.

    1. What's with the colon :?
    2. What's with the spaces?
    3. What's a fork?
    4. What's a join?
    5. What's a URL?

    The C++ equivalent would resemble the following.

    1. What's a const?
    2. What's with the auto thing?
    3. How does assignment work?
    4. What's with the Task?
    5. What's with the double colons ::?
    6. What's a join?
    7. Why the parenthesis?
    8. What's that m_Factories thing?
    9. Why does m_Factories start with m_?
    10. What's with the . after m_factories?
    11. Why can't we use . after HTTP, and what's with the -> part?
    12. What does Start do?
    13. What's with the semicolons?

    Facts are, once you break down C++ into questions required to have an answer to before becoming productive in the language, C++ is little more than "glorified regex", with millions of characters, tied together such that changing just a single one of these characters, typically have repercussions to your entire program. The same is also true for most other OOP languages ...
item.author: polterguy

item.title: 成对的钩子图案 | The paired hook pattern
item.description:
在使用 React 和 TypeScript 多年之后，我看到了很多组件开发模式，但到目前为止，我还没有看到像“配对钩子模式”那样对函数组件有效的模式。 首先，让我们使用一个经典的： Counter组件。

一个简单的例子

首先我们编写一个无状态组件：

 const Counter = ({ count , onDecrement , onIncrement }) => ( <> < span > { count } </ span > < button onClick = { onIncrement } > + </ button > < button onClick = { onDecrement } > - </ button > </> );

而当我们使用它的时候，我们需要为它创建一个状态：

 const App = () => { const [ count , setCount ] = useState ( 0 ); return ( < Counter count = { count } onDecrement = { () => setCount ( count - 1 ) } onIncrement = { () => setCount ( count + 1 ) } /> ); };

动态看起来像这样：

第一个问题：重用

无状态组件的问题在于，我们每次使用组件时都需要使用useState钩子，这对于需要更多属性且遍布整个应用程序的组件来说可能会很烦人。

因此，将状态直接放在组件中是很常见的。 这样做我们不需要每次使用它时都有一个状态，所以我们的Counter组件会变成这样：

 const Counter = ({ initialCount = 0 , step = 1 }) => { const [ count , setCount ] = useState ( initialCount ); return ( <> < span > { count } </ span > < button onClick = { () => setCount ( count + step ) } > + </ button > < button onClick = { () => setCount ( count - step ) } > - </ button > </> ); };

然后使用它，我们想使用多少次就多少次，而不必为每个创建一个状态：

 const App = () => ( <> < Counter /> < Counter /> < Counter /> </> );

然后动态看起来像这样：

第二个问题：数据流

现在，这很好，直到我们想从父元素中知道计数器元素的当前状态。 所以你可能会想创造一个像这样的怪物：

 const Counter = ({ initialCount = 0 , step = 1 , onCountChange }) => { const [ count , setCount ] = useState ( initialCount ); useEffect (() => onCountChange ?.( count ), [ count ]); return ( <> < span > { count } </ span > < button onClick = { () => setCount ( count + step ) } > + </ button > < button onClick = { () => setCount ( count - step ) } > - </ button > </> ); };

然后像这样使用它：

 const App = () => { const [ count , setCount ] = useState ( 0 ); return ( <> < span > Current count in Counter: { count } </ span > < Counter onCountChange = { setCount } /> </> ); };

一开始可能并不明显，但我们为每个状态更改引入了副作用，只是为了让父级与子级保持同步，这有两个重要问题：

  1. 状态同时存在于两个地方（父元素和子元素）。
  2. 孩子们正在更新父母的状态，所以我们实际上是在反对单向数据流。

钩子最好的事情之一就是我们创建自己的钩子。 我为这个问题提出的解决方案非常简单，但老实说，我相信可以解决我所见过的绝大多数状态问题。 第一步和我们在这里开始的类似，我们只是创建一个无状态组件：

 const Counter = ({ count , onDecrement , onIncrement }) => ( <> < span > { count } </ span > < button onClick = { onIncrement } > + </ button > < button onClick = { onDecrement } > - </ button > </> );

但是这一次，我们不再要求我们组件的消费者自己弄清楚状态，而是创建了一个与我们的组件一起使用的钩子，我们可以将其useCounter 。 这个钩子的主要要求是它需要返回一个属性与Counter的属性匹配的对象：

 const useCounter = ({ initialCount = 0 , step = 1 } = {}) => { const [ count , setCount ] = useState ( initialCount ); return useMemo ( () => ({ count , onDecrement : () => setCount ( count - step ), onIncrement : () => setCount ( count + step ), }), [ count , step ], ); };

这使得现在我们几乎可以将它用作有状态的组件：

 const App = () => { const counterProps = useCounter (); return < Counter { ... counterProps } />; };

但我们也可以将其用作无状态组件：

 const App = () => < Counter count = { 42 } />;

而且我们不再有访问状态的限制，因为状态实际上是在父级中。

 const App = () => { const { count , ... counterProps } = useCounter (); return ( <> < span > Current count in Counter: { count } </ span > < Counter { ...{ count , ... counterProps } } /> </> ); };

然后动态看起来像这样：

通过这种方法，我们真正使我们的组件可重用，因为它不需要上下文或基于副作用的奇怪回调或类似的东西。 我们只有一个很好的纯无状态组件，如果我们想控制任何特定的属性，我们可以直接或部分传递一个钩子。

配对模式的问题（和解决方案）

配对钩子方法的主要问题是，现在我们需要为每个具有某种状态的组件创建一个钩子，当我们只有一个组件时这很好，但是当我们有多个相同类型的组件时就变得棘手了（例如有一个Counter组件列表）。

你可能会想做这样的事情：

 const App = ({ list }) => ( <> { list . map ( initialCount => { const counterProps = useCounter ({ initialCount }); return < Counter { ... counterProps } />; }) } </> );

但是这种方法的问题是你违反了钩子的规则，因为你在循环中调用了useCounter钩子。 现在，如果您考虑一下，您可以遍历具有自己状态的组件，因此一种可行的解决方案是创建组件的“配对”版本，它会为您调用钩子：

 const PairedCounter = ({ initialCount , step , ... props }) => { const counterProps = useCounter ({ initialCount , step }); return < Counter { ... counterProps } { ... props } />; }; // And then... const App = ({ list }) => ( <> { list . map ( initialCount => ( < PairedCounter initialCount = { initialCount } /> )) } </> );

这种方法看起来类似于有状态方法（本文中的第二个示例），但更加灵活和可测试。 我们的另一种方法是为每个项目创建一个组件上下文，而不必自己编写组件，为此，我创建了一个小函数，我在 npm 中发布了一个名为react-pair的函数：

这个函数很简单，你可以自己写，唯一的区别是我在测试它，添加 devtools 集成，并为你使用 TypeScript 输入。 您可以在此处查看来源。 用法很简单， react-pair提供了一个pair函数，你可以用它来创建一个组件，让你在组件上下文中访问钩子（不破坏钩子的规则）：

 import { pair } from " react-pair " ; import { useCounter } from " ./useCounter " ; const PairedCounter = pair ( useCounter ); const Component = ({ list }) => ( < ul > { array . map (( initialCount , index ) => ( < PairedCounter key = { index } > { usePairedCounter => { const counterProps = usePairedCounter ({ initialCount }); return < Counter { ... counterProps } />; } } </ PairedCounter > )) } </ ul > );

需要说明的是，您不需要使用react-pair来实现这一点，您只需手动创建一个新的有状态组件，只需将钩子与组件配对即可。

无论您是否使用 util，生成的动态看起来像这样：

编写一个无状态组件，设计为独立工作。
  • 编写一个自定义钩子以与该组件配对。
  • 使用带有钩子的组件来获得有状态的体验。
  • 使用没有钩子的组件来获得无状态体验。
  • 使用仅具有来自钩子的几个属性的组件以获得混合体验。
  • 循环时使用 util 或 wrapper 组件。

    • 结束的想法

    我已经使用这种模式有一段时间了，到目前为止我没有发现任何阻塞问题，所以我邀请你在你的一个项目中尝试它并告诉我它是如何进行的！



    原文:

    After years of working with React and TypeScript, I've seen a lot of patterns for component development, but so far I didn't see one that works as good for function components as the "paired hook pattern". To get started let's use a classic: The Counter component.

    A simple example

    First we write a stateless component:

    const Counter = ({ count, onDecrement, onIncrement }) => ( <> <span>{count}</span> <button onClick={onIncrement}>+</button> <button onClick={onDecrement}>-</button> </>
);

    And when we use it, we need to create a state for it:

    const App = () => { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ( <Counter count={count} onDecrement={() => setCount(count - 1)} onIncrement={() => setCount(count + 1)} /> );
};

    The dynamic looks something like this:

    The first problem: Reuse

    The problem with the stateless component is that we need to use the useState hook every time we use the component, which might be annoying for components that require more properties and are all over your app.

    So, is pretty common to just put the state directly in the component. Doing this we don't need to have a state every time we use it, so then our Counter component changes to something like this:

    const Counter = ({ initialCount = 0, step = 1 }) => { const [count, setCount] = useState(initialCount); return ( <> <span>{count}</span> <button onClick={() => setCount(count + step)}>+</button> <button onClick={() => setCount(count - step)}>-</button> </> );
};

    And then to use it, as many times as we want without having to create a state for each:

    const App = () => ( <> <Counter /> <Counter /> <Counter /> </>
);

    The dynamic then looks like this:

    The second problem: Data flow

    Now, that's great until we want to know the current state of the counter element from the parent element. So you might be tempted to create a monster like this one:

    const Counter = ({ initialCount = 0, step = 1, onCountChange }) => { const [count, setCount] = useState(initialCount); useEffect(() => onCountChange?.(count), [count]); return ( <> <span>{count}</span> <button onClick={() => setCount(count + step)}>+</button> <button onClick={() => setCount(count - step)}>-</button> </> );
};

    And then use it like this:

    const App = () => { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ( <> <span>Current count in Counter: {count}</span> <Counter onCountChange={setCount} /> </> );
};

    It might not be obvious at first, but we are introducing side effects to every state change just to keep the parent in sync with the children, and this has two significant issues:

    1. The state is living in two places at once (the parent element and the children).
    2. The children are updating the state of the parent, so we are effectively going against the one-way data flow.

    One of the best things about hooks is when we create our own. The solution I propose for this issue is quite simple, but I honestly believe solves the vast majority of issues with state I've seen around. The first step is similar to what we had at the beginning here, we just create a stateless component:

    const Counter = ({ count, onDecrement, onIncrement }) => ( <> <span>{count}</span> <button onClick={onIncrement}>+</button> <button onClick={onDecrement}>-</button> </>
);

    But this time, instead of requiring the consumers of our component to figure out the state themselves, we create a hook that goes together with our component, we can call it useCounter. The main requirement for this hook is that it needs to return an object with properties matching the properties of Counter:

    const useCounter = ({ initialCount = 0, step = 1 } = {}) => { const [count, setCount] = useState(initialCount); return useMemo( () => ({ count, onDecrement: () => setCount(count - step), onIncrement: () => setCount(count + step), }), [count, step], );
};

    What this enables is that now we can use it almost as a stateful component:

    const App = () => { const counterProps = useCounter(); return <Counter {...counterProps} />;
};

    But also we can use it as a stateless component:

    const App = () => <Counter count={42} />;

    And we no longer have limitations accessing the state, because the state is actually in the parent.

    const App = () => { const { count, ...counterProps } = useCounter(); return ( <> <span>Current count in Counter: {count}</span> <Counter {...{ count, ...counterProps }} /> </> );
};

    The dynamic then looks something like this:

    With this approach, we are truly making our component reusable by not making it require a context or weird callbacks based on side effects or anything like that. We just have a nice pure stateless component, with a hook that we can pass directly or just partially if we want to take control of any property in particular.

    The name "paired hook" then comes from providing a hook with a stateless component that can be paired to it.

    A problem (and solution) with the paired pattern

    The main issue the paired hook approach has is that now we need a hook for every component with some kind of state, which is fine when we have a single component, but becomes tricky when we have several components of the same type (like for example having a list of Counter components).

    You might be tempted to do something like this:

    const App = ({ list }) => ( <> {list.map(initialCount => { const counterProps = useCounter({ initialCount }); return <Counter {...counterProps} />; })} </>
);

    But the problem with this approach is that you're going against the rules of hooks because you're calling the useCounter hook inside a loop. Now, if you think about it, you can loop over components that have their own state, so one viable solution is to create a "paired" version of your component, which calls the hook for you:

    const PairedCounter = ({ initialCount, step, ...props }) => { const counterProps = useCounter({ initialCount, step }); return <Counter {...counterProps} {...props} />;
}; // And then...
const App = ({ list }) => ( <> {list.map(initialCount => ( <PairedCounter initialCount={initialCount} /> ))} </>
);

    This approach seems similar to the stateful approach (the second example in this article) but is way more flexible and testable. The other approach we have is to create a component context for every item without having to write a component ourselves, and for that, I created a small function that I published in npm called react-pair:

    The function is so simple, you could write it yourself, the only difference is that I'm testing it, adding devtools integration, and typing with TypeScript for you. You can check the source here. The usage is quite simple, react-pair provides a pair function that you can use to create a component that gives you access to the hook in a component context (without breaking the rules of hooks):

    import { pair } from "react-pair";
import { useCounter } from "./useCounter"; const PairedCounter = pair(useCounter); const Component = ({ list }) => ( <ul> {array.map((initialCount, index) => ( <PairedCounter key={index}> {usePairedCounter => { const counterProps = usePairedCounter({ initialCount }); return <Counter {...counterProps} />; }} </PairedCounter> ))} </ul>
);

    Just to be clear, you don't need to use react-pair to achieve this, you can just create a new stateful component by hand, that just pairs the hook with the component.

    Either if you use the util or not, the resulting dynamic looks something like this:

    TL;DR

    • Write a stateless component, designed to work in isolation.
    • Write a custom hook to be paired with that component.
    • Use the component with the hook for a stateful experience.
    • Use the component without the hook for a stateless experience.
    • Use the component with just a few properties from the hook for a mixed experience.
    • Use an util or a wrapper component when looping.

    Closing thoughts

    I've been using this pattern for a while now and so far I didn't found any blocking issues with it, so I invite you to try it out in one of your projects and tell me how it goes!

item.title: 介绍 Mayfield + DEV 讨论系列 | Introducing the Mayfield + DEV discussion series
item.description:
嘿开发社区！

在接下来的两周内，我们将与我们在Mayfield的 DEV 合作伙伴合作举办每日系列讨论会。

自然地，我们觉得一些#discuss线程是完美的出路。

从今天晚些时候开始，我们将在接下来的两周内每个工作日发布一个讨论（总共 10 个帖子）。 它们只是普通的讨论线程，就像每天在 DEV 上发布的那样，但我们希望得到一些很好的答案来深入研究和更广泛地分享。

我们非常期待人们有什么要说的！ 寻找今天晚些时候发布的第一个帖子。

快乐编码！



原文:

Hey DEV Community!

Over the next two weeks, we will be hosting a daily discussion series in collaboration with our DEV partners at Mayfield.

Mayfield is deeply invested in dev-centric companies such as HashiCorp — and they came to us hoping to learn from the community on topics such as the best perceived companies to work for and to get into the nuance of what developers need and want most out of their careers these days. Naturally, we felt like some #discuss threads were the perfect outlet.

Starting later today, we will be posting one discussion per weekday every for the next two weeks (10 posts total). They are just normal discussion threads, like are posted every day here on DEV, but we hope to get some great answers to dive into and share more broadly.

We are very much looking forward to what people have to say! Look for the first post coming out later today.

Happy coding!
item.author: ben

item.title: 静态类型与动态类型：差异解释 | Static Typing VS Dynamic Typing: The Difference Explained
item.description:
阿琼女士

发表于您可能已经听说过在讨论编程语言时出现的术语“静态类型”和“动态类型”。 但它们是什么意思，为什么知道它很重要？

要了解静态类型和动态类型之间的区别，我们先来谈谈类型检查。 类型检查是编译器或解释器使用的一个过程，以确保以每种语言的正确语法编写程序。 静态类型检查将在您使用编译器编写代码时识别任何错误，而动态类型检查将在运行时识别任何错误。

什么是静态打字？

静态类型是许多编程语言中使用的一种约定，用于定义代码中变量的类型。 变量的类型是在编译时确定的（程序代码转换为机器代码的时间段）。

静态类型对开发人员来说可能是有益的，因为它可以让他们更容易地理解他们正在编写的代码，并且可以帮助他们防止错误。 静态类型编程语言的示例包括 C、Java、C++、Scala 等……

简单来说，静态类型意味着您在程序启动之前告诉计算机程序中的数据类型。

静态类型的优点

类型安全有助于防止意外的类型转换（例如，数字类型之间），这可能会产生不正确的结果或导致运行时错误。 静态类型使新开发人员更容易理解和修改现有代码，因为可以通过分析源代码本身轻松确定每个变量的预期类型。

它可以帮助在开发周期的早期检测错误。 编译器或解释器在运行代码之前检查类型错误。 这意味着您可以在编译时发现错误，而不是在运行时代码崩溃时感到惊讶。

什么是动态类型？

动态类型是一种在许多编程语言和工具中使用的技术，是一种确定在运行时（即代码运行时）存储的数据类型的方法。 与静态类型不同，其中所有变量都提前键入并在整个程序执行过程中保持不变，动态类型允许变量的数据类型在执行期间更改。 这很有用，因为它允许程序员在同一个变量中存储不同类型的数据； 但是，它也可能导致代码中出现意外错误。

简而言之，动态类型是一种类型，您不必在创建变量时决定变量的类型。

动态类型编程语言的示例包括 JavaScript、Python、PHP、Ruby 等……

动态类型的优点

更容易写。 声明变量类型可能很痛苦，尤其是在需要多次声明相同类型的多行语句中。 更容易重构代码。 重命名动态类型的变量不需要更改它们的声明或类型注释； 使用静态类型，您必须更改代码库中变量类型的所有引用。

更快的开发时间。 无需声明变量类型，您可以简单地开始编码，然后运行您的代码。

结论

不可否认，静态类型非常强大，它能够避免运行时错误。 动态类型更容易集成，并且不需要对现有代码进行大量更改。 哪一个赢了？ 这取决于您希望如何处理您的项目。 明智地选择——你今天选择的类型系统会在未来几年影响你的代码。

克里斯蒂安·费尔南多—— 雅库布·安德杰耶夫斯基—— 阿卜杜拉·帕拉查—— Apr 27 4月27日



原文:

Arjun M S

Posted on May 10

You’ve probably heard the terms “static typing” and “dynamic typing” thrown around in discussions of programming languages. But what do they mean, and why is it important to know?

To understand the difference between static and dynamic typing, let’s first talk about type checking. Type checking is a process used by compilers or interpreters to ensure that a program is written with correct syntax for each language. Static type checking will identify any errors when you’re writing code using a compiler, whereas dynamic type checking will identify any errors at runtime.

What is Static Typing?

Static typing is a convention used in many programming languages that defines the type of variable in the code. The type of a variable is determined at compile time(the period when the programming code is converted to the machine code).

Static typing can be beneficial for developers because it allows them to more easily understand the code they are writing and it can help them to prevent errors. Examples of statically typed programming languages include C, Java, C++,Scala, etc…

In simple words, Static typing means you tell the computer what type of data is in the program before the program starts.

Example in Java:

String x = “Hello world!”; // x is a string
int y = 15;  // y is an integer

Advantages of Static Typing

Type safety can help prevent unintended type conversions (for example, between numeric types), which can produce incorrect results or cause runtime errors. Static typing makes it easier for a new developer to understand and modify existing code, as the expected types of each variable can easily be determined through analysis of the source code itself.

It can help detect bugs earlier in the development cycle. The compiler or interpreter checks for type errors before running your code. This means you can find errors at compile time instead of being surprised at runtime when your code crashes.

What is Dynamic Typing?

Dynamic typing, a technique used in many programming languages and tools, is a method of determining the type of data being stored at runtime (i.e., when code is running). In contrast to static typing, where all variables are typed ahead of time and remain constant throughout a program’s execution, dynamic typing allows the data type of a variable to change during execution. This can be useful because it allows programmers to store different types of data in the same variable; however, it can also lead to unexpected errors in code.

In simple words, Dynamic typing is a type of typing where you don’t have to decide what the type of a variable is when you make the variable.

Examples of dynamically typed programming languages include JavaScript,Python, PHP,Ruby, etc…

Example in Python:

$ name = “John Doe” // $name is now of type string
$ age = 15; // $age is now an integer

Advantages of Dynamic Typing

Easier to write. Declaring variable types can be a pain, especially in multi-line statements when you need to declare the same type multiple times. Easier to refactor code. Renaming variables that are dynamically typed does not require changing their declaration or type annotation; with static typing, you would have to change all references in the codebase for the variable’s type.

Faster development time. Without needing to declare variable types, you can simply start coding and then run your code.

Conclusion

Static typing is undeniably powerful, with its ability to avoid runtime errors. Dynamic typing is easier to integrate, and doesn’t require extensive changes to existing code. Which one wins? It depends on how you want to approach your project. Choose wisely — the type system you choose today can affect your code for years to come.

Cristian Fernando - Apr 27

Jakub Andrzejewski - Apr 27

Abdullah Paracha - Apr 27
item.author: arjunms

item.title: 在 AWS EC2 实例 (Ubuntu) 上安装 VPN (OpenVPN) 服务器 | Install VPN (OpenVPN) Server on AWS EC2 Instance (Ubuntu)
item.description:
ピメン.ヴィ.ブリタニア

发表于我认为您可以使用 ec2 免费层来创建自己的 vpn 服务器，而不是购买或使用免费的受限 vpn，嗯...

首先，您需要登录到您的 aws 控制台并找到 ec2 服务，然后创建一个新实例（启动实例）：
选择免费套餐实例类型

  • 生成您自己的密钥对（需要与 ssh 连接）
  • 在网络设置上单击edit
    • 单击add security group rule ，然后为自定义 UDP 添加新规则，端口范围 = 1194，源类型来自anywhere
  • 然后在Advanced detail设置滚动到底部，您将找到User data字段，并填写：
    •  #! /bin/bash apt-get update curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/angristan/openvpn-install/master/openvpn-install.sh chmod +x openvpn-install.sh APPROVE_INSTALL=y ENDPOINT=$(curl -4 ifconfig.co) APPROVE_IP=y IPV6_SUPPORT=n PORT_CHOICE=1 PROTOCOL_CHOICE=1 DNS=1 COMPRESSION_ENABLED=n CUSTOMIZE_ENC=n CLIENT=david PASS=1 ./openvpn-install.sh

    创建实例后，您可以使用您的实例key-pair.pem登录到实例，然后您可以安装openvpn-install以生成您的帐户/配置文件 (.ovpn)。

    安装后，您可以通过运行生成文件：

     sudo ./openvpn-install.sh

    您可以将配置文件复制到本地计算机，然后安装 OpenVpn 客户端 (windows) 或 Tunnelblick (MacOs)，然后连接！



    原文:

    ピメン.ヴィ.ブリタニア

    Posted on May 10

    I think you can use ec2 free tier to create your own vpn server instead of buying or using free limited vpn, hm...

    First, you need to login to your aws console and find ec2 service, then create a new instance (launch intance):
    ***

    • Select free tier instance type
    • Generate your own key-pair (required to connect with ssh)
    • On network setting click edit
      • Click add security group rule then add new rule for custom UDP with port range = 1194 and source type from anywhere
    • Then on Advanced detail setting scroll to bottom and you will find User data field, and fill with: 
    #! /bin/bash
apt-get update
curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/angristan/openvpn-install/master/openvpn-install.sh

chmod +x openvpn-install.sh

APPROVE_INSTALL=y ENDPOINT=$(curl -4 ifconfig.co) APPROVE_IP=y IPV6_SUPPORT=n PORT_CHOICE=1 PROTOCOL_CHOICE=1 DNS=1 COMPRESSION_ENABLED=n  CUSTOMIZE_ENC=n CLIENT=david PASS=1 ./openvpn-install.sh

    After instance created, you can login into instance with your instance key-pair.pem, then you can install openvpn-install to generate your account/configuration file (.ovpn).

    When installed, you can generate file by running:

    sudo ./openvpn-install.sh

    You can copy the configuration file into your local machine, and install OpenVpn client (windows) or Tunnelblick (MacOs), then CONNECT!!
item.author: pimenvibritania

item.title: #08：建立博客 - 使用 Astro！ | #08: Build a Blog - With Astro!
item.description:
欢迎来到#30DaysOfSWA Week 2, Day 1 ！

在第 1 周，我们介绍了 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序的核心概念——从开发到 API 启用、安全性、配置和部署。

今天，我们开始第 2 周，重点关注 SWA 的实际应用——使用流行的 Web 开发框架和静态站点生成器来构建和部署不同类型的应用程序。 我们首先看一下Astro - 一个采用 BYOF（“自带框架”）方法的现代静态站点生成器。

我们将涵盖的内容

  • 什么是静态站点生成器？
  • 什么是天文？
  • 使用 Astro 构建和部署个人博客
  • 练习：尝试使用教程来部署您的个人博客！

静态站点生成器

术语“静态站点生成器”(SSG) 是指自动为您构建静态网页、使用您的自定义内容或数据填充所选模板的工具。 代码生成对您来说是透明的，即使是非开发人员也可以快速设置自定义静态网站。

静态网站生成是快速、灵活的网站创建的发展趋势。 但它需要一个外部服务来处理托管和构建/部署工作流。 这就是 Azure 静态 Web 应用的用武之地！ 查看SWA 文档，了解有关 Hugo、Jekyll 和 Gatsby 等流行 SSG 的教程。 我们将在本周晚些时候探讨其他一些示例。

你好，阿斯特罗！

然而，今天的帖子将重点关注Astro ，它是静态站点类别中 JavaScript 的后起之秀之一。 自带框架（React、Vue、Svelte、Web 组件）

  • 使用该框架的 UI 组件编写您的网站
  • 以下是 Astro 的简要概述。

    天文 + SWA

    在今天的教程中，我们将逐步完成以下步骤：

    最终结果将如下所示。

    以下是要了解的关键链接：

    然后明天再回来查看新的使用示例和教程——这一次，重点是 React——我们将继续探索 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序的实际应用！

    锻炼

    这里有几个选项可以尝试，以获得更多的实践经验：

    • 克隆模板项目并按照教程构建和部署您的个人博客。
    • 访问Astro 主题页面并尝试使用不同的启动项目，然后将其部署到 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序。

    资源

    查看使用更传统的静态站点生成器的其他教程：



    • 原文:

    Welcome to Week 2, Day 1 of #30DaysOfSWA!!

    In Week 1 we covered core concepts for Azure Static Web Apps - from development to API-enablement, security, configuraton and deployment.

    Today, we kick off Week 2 with a focus on seeing SWA in action - using popular web development frameworks and static site generators to build and deploy different kinds of applications. We kick things off with a look at Astro - a modern static site generator with a BYOF ("Bring your own framework") approach.

    What We'll Cover

    • What is a static site generator?
    • What is Astro?
    • Build & Deploy a Personal Blog with Astro
    • Exercise: Try the tutorial to deploy your personal blog!

    Static Site Generators

    The term "static site generators" (SSG) refers to tools that automatically build static webpages for you, populating a chosen template with your custom content or data. The code generation is transparent to you, making it possible for even non-developers to setup custom static websites quickly.

    Static site generation is a growing trend for fast, flexible website creation. But it requires an external service to handle hosting and build/deploy workflows. This is where Azure Static Web Apps comes in! Check out the SWA documentation for tutorials featuring popular SSGs like Hugo, Jekyll and Gatsby. We'll explore some other examples later this week.

    Hello, Astro!

    Today's post will however focus on Astro, one of the rising stars of JavaScript in the Static Sites category. Astro is a static site builder that works like an SSG but with some novel features that set it apart.

    • Bring your own framework (React, Vue, Svelte, web components)
    • Compose your website using that framework's UI components
    • Astro now renders a fully-static (all HTML, no JavaScript) website

    Astro emphasizes fast performance with less JavaScript - and takes a partial hydration approach that allows it to load the minimal required amount of JavaScript, if and when absolutely necessary. Here's a quick overview of Astro.

    Astro + SWA

    In today's tutorial, we walk through the steps required to:

    The end result will look like this.

    Here are the key links to know:

    Then check back tomorrow for a new usage example and tutorial - this time, with a focus on React - as we continue to explore Azure Static Web Apps in action!

    Exercise

    Here are a couple of options to try, to get more hands-on experience:

    • Clone the template project and follow the tutorial to build and deploy your personal blog.
    • Visit the Astro Themes page and try using a different starter project, then deploying it to Azure Static Web Apps.

    Resources

    Check out these other tutorials using more traditional static site generators:

item.title: 如何做困难的事情 | How to Do Hard Things
item.description:
通往成功的道路不是秘密，这是一个令人不快的事实。 我们大多数人都清楚地知道我们需要做什么来改善我们的生活。 但我们不做这些事情，因为它们很难。 我们更喜欢技巧、公式和技巧，而不是做我们知道最有效的一件事。 几个例子：

  • 我们报名参加课程，而不是从头开始编写网站。
  • 我们做孤立的机器练习，而不是在健身房里举重。
  • 我们忽略了我们的伴侣，而不是进行艰难的交谈。
  • 我们继续时尚饮食，而不是建立健康的生活方式。
  • 我们滚动推特而不是看书。

我们这样做是有原因的，这就是所谓的享乐动机。 人类天生倾向于喜欢感觉良好的事物并避免感觉不好的事物。 这就是我们在网上搜索生产力技巧而不是真正提高生产力的原因。 我们也将这些选择合理化，通常以非常聪明的方式，但在内心深处，我们可以感觉到我们只是在避免做有效但困难的事情。

如何做困难的事情

弄清楚困难是什么

首先，你需要确保你知道在你想要提高的技能或工作中什么是困难但有效的事情。 这应该很容易弄清楚，因为它通常是创建输出的东西。 对于作家来说，就是写作。 对于编码员来说，就是编码。 对于音乐家来说，它正在写一首歌。 对于运动员来说，它正在锻炼身体。

重新连接你的大脑

花更多时间在有效、困难的事情上的诀窍是改变你的想法，让困难的事情看起来不错，而替代的事情看起来很糟糕。 例如，您可以从头开始编写一个网站并真正成为一名更有能力的开发人员，或者您可以注册另一门课程，重新学习您已经知道的内容或您可以轻松查找的内容。 哪一个更好？

找出你的原因

确保你知道你为什么要做这件艰难的事情。 如果你不知道为什么，你很可能很快就会失去动力。 所以找出你的原因并经常提醒自己。 把它写下来，放在你能看到的地方。 你的为什么越强，越好。

为它腾出时间

接下来，做出承诺。 把困难的事情变成习惯。 每天在特定的时间，你都会做这件艰难的事情。 纯粹的专注，没有一心多用。 一开始放轻松，然后逐渐延长你花在这上面的时间。 享受它，也是。 享受磨砺，痛苦，困难。 你是赢家，你可以做到。

抛开疑虑

当你做某件困难的事情时，你的大脑会想方设法阻止它。 你会想查找或找到更有效的方法来完成困难的事情。 在你努力工作的过程中，不要屈服于这些诱人的论点。 在之后或之前做任何你想做的事，但在此期间保持专注。

如果坚持不懈地努力工作，您不仅会在人群中脱颖而出，而且还会增加运气的表面积。 如果您从不发布任何代码，招聘人员将永远不会与您联系并提供您梦寐以求的技术工作。 但如果你努力工作并在公共场合展示这项工作，他们可能会这样做。

任何事情都一样。 如果您通过做艰苦的事情来不断提高自己，那么这将产生的机会数量只会越来越多。 所以你会在你的目标技能上变得更好，并且在这个过程中似乎也会成为一个更幸运的人。 所以不要花太多时间来解决艰巨的任务。 只是做困难的事情。



原文:

It's an inconvenient truth that the road to success isn't a secret. Most of us know exactly what we need to do to improve our lives. But we don't do those things because they are hard. We prefer tricks, formulas, and hacks over doing the one thing that we know is most effective. A few examples:

  • We sign up for a course instead of coding a website from scratch.
  • We do isolated machine exercises instead of heavy lifts in the gym.
  • We ignore our partner instead of having a hard conversation.
  • We go on fad diets instead of building a healthy lifestyle.
  • We scroll Twitter instead of reading a book.

There's a reason we act like this and it's called hedonic motivation. Humans are wired to prefer things that feel good and avoid things that feel bad. That's why we search for productivity tips online instead of actually being productive. We rationalize these choices too, often in very clever ways, but deep down we can feel that we're simply avoiding doing what's effective, but hard.

How to Do the Hard Things

Figure Out What the Hard Thing Is

First, you need to make sure you know what the hard, but effective thing is in the skill or job that you want to improve. This should be easy to figure out, because it's usually the thing that creates the output. For writers, it's writing. For coders, it's coding. For musicians, it's writing a song. For athletes, it's doing a workout.

Rewire Your Brain

The trick to spending more time on the effective, hard things is to change your thinking so the hard thing looks good while the alternative looks bad. For example, you can program a website from scratch and genuinely become a more competent developer or you can sign up for another course that will rehash what you already know or what you could easily have looked up. Which one is better?

Figure Out Your Why

Make sure you know why you want to do this hard thing. If you don't know why, chances are you'll lose motivation pretty quickly. So figure out your why and frequently remind yourself of it. Write it down and put it where you can see it. The stronger your why, the better.

Make Time for It

Next up, make a commitment. Turn the hard thing into a habit. Every day at a particular hour, you will do this one hard thing. Pure focus, no multi-tasking. Take it easy at first and gradually lengthen the amount of time you spend on this. Enjoy it, too. Enjoy the grind, the pain, the difficulty. You're a winner and you can do it.

Leave the Doubts Behind

When you're doing something hard, your brain will try to find ways to stop doing it. You'll want to look things up or find more efficient ways of doing the hard thing. Don't give in to these seductive arguments during your hard work sessions. Do whatever you want after or before, but stay focused during.

If consistently put in the hard work, you will not only stand out from the crowd, but you will also increase your surface area for luck. There's no way a recruiter will ever reach out to you with your dream job in tech if you never publish any code. But if you work hard and show that work in public, they just might.

The same goes for anything. If you keep improving yourself by doing hard things, the number of opportunities that this will generate will only ever go up. So you'll be getting better at your targeted skill and seemingly become a luckier person in the process too. So don't spend too much time working around the hard task. Just do the hard thing instead.
